# ALF — Azail Layer Format
> Machine-readable summary for LLM indexing agents. Full specification: https://dotalf.com/spec/v1

## What is ALF
ALF (Azail Layer Format) is an open JSON file format (.alf extension) for encoding portable, model-neutral expert reasoning layers. An ALF file tells a language model how a domain expert thinks. It works with Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, and any other language model. It is not a model, not a fine-tune, and not a prompt template.

## The nine sections (all required)
1. metadata — identity, layer_type, display_type, attribution, licence
2. trigger_conditions — when the layer activates (min 2, max 10)
3. required_inputs — context the layer needs to fire (min 2, max 8)
4. enhancement_templates — instructions that reshape model behaviour (min 5, max 20)
5. conversation_map — multi-turn step sequences (min 2, max 10)
6. blind_spots — gaps the non-expert does not know they have (min 3, max 12)
7. error_correction_patterns — common mistakes and corrections (min 2, max 10)
8. unknown_unknowns — named insights an expert raises that the non-expert never thinks to ask (min 4, max 12)
9. search_tags — primary (min 8), secondary (min 6), contextual (min 5)

## Four layer types
- reasoning: encodes how an expert evaluates a situation
- procedure: encodes a sequence with a defined correct order
- context: carries standing facts, precedents, or constraints
- data: pointer to external runtime data (data never enters the file)

## Unknown unknowns format rule
Every unknown_unknowns entry label must follow: "On [3-5 words] —"
Example: "On anchoring in early diagnosis —"
Content must open with the expert insight or non-expert assumption being challenged, not a definition.

## Conformance
A conformant ALF v1 file: valid JSON, all 9 sections present, all minimum counts met, metadata.alf_version is "1.0", metadata.layer_id is UUID v4, layer_type and display_type are valid enum values.

## The open/private boundary
The format spec is open — no licence required to implement a reader, writer, or validator.
Protection of commercial layers is structural, not cryptographic.
ALF v1 defines no encryption-at-rest or watermarking.

## File details
- Extension: .alf
- Encoding: UTF-8
- Format: JSON (RFC 8259)
- MIME type: application/vnd.azail.alf+json
- Max size: 512 KB

## Links
- Full spec: https://dotalf.com/spec/v1
- JSON Schema: https://dotalf.com/spec/v1/schema.json
- GitHub: https://github.com/phihal00/alf-spec
- Reference implementation: https://azail.ai
- Maintained by: Azail AB
